zh.lifeSummary: 當Weinemar de GAND於1070年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特時，他的父親Lambert II de GAND21歲，母親Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer18歲。他於1106年與Gisèle de GUÎNES在比利時福拉安德倫結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1120年在他的家鄉享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Weinemar de GAND于1070年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特时，他的父亲Lambert II de GAND21岁，母亲Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer18岁。他于1106年与Gisèle de GUÎNES在比利时福拉安德倫结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1120年在他的家乡享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: GAND
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

en.lifeSummary: When Weinemar de GAND was born in 1070, in Ghent, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Lambert II de GAND, was 21 and his mother, Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, was 18. He married Gisèle de GUÎNES in 1106, in Flanders, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1120, in his hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Weinemar de GAND est né en 1070, à Gand, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Lambert II de GAND, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Gisèle de GUÎNES en 1106, à Flandre, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1120, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Weinemar de GAND 1070 in Gent, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lambert II de GAND 21 und seine Mutter Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer 18. Er hat 1106 in Vlaanderen, Belgien Gisèle de GUÎNES geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1120 im Alter von 50 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Weinemar de GAND è nato il nel 1070 nel Gent, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Lambert II de GAND, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Gisèle de GUÎNES nel 1106, nel Vlaanderen, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1120, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Weinemar de GANDが1070年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンジェントで生まれた時，父Lambert II de GANDは21歳，母Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omerは18歳であった。1106年にベルギーvラアンデレンでGisèle de GUÎNESと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1120年に故郷で50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Weinemar de GAND(이)가 1070년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 젠트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lambert II de GAND(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1106년 벨기에 블라아더에서 Gisèle de GUÎNES(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1120년 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Weinemar de GAND nasceu em 1070, em Gent, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Lambert II de GAND, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Gisèle de GUÎNES em 1106, em Vlaanderen, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1120, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Weinemar de GAND родился в 1070 г. в местности джент, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Lambert II de GAND было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Gisèle de GUÎNES в 1106 г. в местности влаандирен, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1120 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Weinemar de GAND nació en 1070, en Gent, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Lambert II de GAND, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Gisèle de GUÎNES en 1106, en Vlaanderen, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1120, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años.
